unit: Unit 8: Apps and the App Store
title: The App Store
drDigitalIntro: The App Store is Apple's marketplace for Mac apps. Open the App Store app and you'll see thousands of apps: games, productivity tools, photo editors, and more. You can search for specific apps or browse by category. Many apps are free; some cost money. Buy an app once and it's yours forever — or pay a subscription for ongoing access. All apps in the App Store are reviewed by Apple for safety.

playgroundTask.question: Why is the App Store the safest place to get Mac apps?

options.text: Apps are always the cheapest there
Apple reviews every app for security before listing it
It's the only place apps can be downloaded
Apps from the App Store never need updating

drDigitalSuccess: Apple's review process is the key — they check each app for malware and policy violations before it goes live.
drDigitalHint: The App Store is like a trusted store with a strict return policy — only vetted apps get in.
